sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, time_value VARCHAR(8), string_value VARCHAR(100), nullable_time_value TIME, optional_value VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, time_value, string_value, nullable_time_value, optional_value) VALUES (1, '18:40:05', 'aabcdaa', NULL, 'example');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, time_value, string_value, nullable_time_value, optional_value) VALUES (2, '08:15:00', 'abc', '08:15:00', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, time_value, string_value, nullable_time_value, optional_value) VALUES (3, '23:59:59', 'xyzabcxyz', NULL, 'test');
SELECT id, trim(string_value, 'a') AS trimmed_string, time(trim(time_value)) AS converted_time FROM test_data WHERE string_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, time(trim(optional_value, 'ex')) AS optional_trimmed_time, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY time_value) AS time_count FROM test_data WHERE nullable_time_value IS NULL;

SELECT t1.id, AVG(CASE WHEN nullable_time_value IS NOT NULL THEN EXTRACT(HOUR FROM nullable_time_value) ELSE 0 END) AS avg_hour FROM test_data t1 JOIN test_data t2 ON t1.id = t2.id GROUP BY t1.id HAVING AVG(t1.id) > 1;
SELECT time('18:40:05') AS specific_time, trim(t2.string_value, 'ab') AS adjusted_string FROM (SELECT string_value FROM test_data WHERE id = 1) AS t2 JOIN test_data ON test_data.id = t2.id;
SELECT id, trim(string_value, 'xyz') AS trimmed_value FROM test_data WHERE time_value = '23:59:59' AND trimmed_value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_data AS SELECT id, time(trim(time_value, '0')) AS modified_time FROM test_data WHERE length(trim(string_value, 'a')) > 0;
